title: [FIRRTL] Define/fix subAnno Lowering
body:
Lowering of `subAnno` worked for the most part.  However, there was an off-by-one bug related to the behavior of `subAnno` using a width too causing annotations to smear out during lowering (see #1453).  (This normally doesn't cause actual bugs, but happens to crash `GrandCentralTaps`.)

Additionally, the behavior around `subAnno`s that crossed a subindex/subfield boundary wasn't well defined and had very weird behavior.  This changes this to do what I think makes sense: 

  - A `subAnno` of form `[x, x]` will target everything "below" field `x`.  For the case of `[0, 0]`, this means everything.
  - A `subAnno` of form `[x, y]` will target everything from `x` to `y`.  If `x` or `y` are aggregates then it pulls in everything "below" them. If there is a `z` between `x` and `y` that includes things beyond `y`, then that is _not_ pulled in.  

There are a ton of tests that push towards exhaustiveness checking of this.

Note: `subAnno`s crossing a subindex/subfield boundary isn't expected to actually happen (a Target cannot generate such a `subAnno`, but it could be canonicalized into one if such canonicalization existed).

Fixes #1453.
